<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: token-optimization

- 2026-07-08T12:22:32.446Z — chose **Anchor the initiative on the two-lever model and make sofar_get_state the P0 target. Lever A (cost/latency) = prompt caching, largely harness-owned → guidance only. Lever B (context-window occupancy) = progressive disclosure + compact serialization → where sofar's own wins live. get_state today returns the full raw folded InitiativeState (~14.2k tok for harness-build; ~10.1k of it the 57-decision history, unbounded), where the budgeted SessionStart block gives the same orientation in ~1k tok.** over Leading with prompt caching (doesn't reduce occupancy), or rewriting the SessionStart injection (already budgeted to ~1k tok / hard 10k-char cap), or adopting heavy MCP machinery (tool-search/deferred-loading/code-execution — overkill at 7 tools). because Audit + 3 independent web research sweeps converge: caching cuts $/latency but NOT the tokens the model must attend to; the industry (Anthropic context-engineering, claude-mem, Agent Skills, Letta, mem0, Cursor rules) has converged on progressive disclosure — inject a thin index, fetch detail on demand. sofar's one large, unbounded, per-orient occupancy sink is get_state's full-fold dump. A compact-default digest + on-demand section fetch fixes it while keeping the full InitiativeState available for the state server/sync (SPEC contract preserved).
- 2026-07-08T12:50:30.902Z — chose **Reframe around the product's own instruments after the strategy-docs review. Token cost is already a Phase-0-scorecard axis (M3 'redundant work — minutes OR tokens', M6 capture overhead), so the goal = drive DOWN cost (M3/M6) while HOLDING resume quality (M1 re-explanation, M4 dead-end recurrence, M5 consistency). The get_state digest keeps RATIONALE first-class (recent decisions surfaced WITH over/because), makes the record re-injectable IN FULL via view:full, and detail fetchable via section:*. No-regression is validated by an automated resume ablation I own (fresh agent resumes from digest vs full; blind grader scores M1/M4/M5) — the user does not hand-score. Build proceeds step by step, each increment test-gated.** over Treating token reduction as pure plumbing (risk: silently compressing the rationale 'muscle' the product's central bet D7 rests on), asserting no-regression without measuring it, or asking the user to hand-score the scorecard. because Spine 'the one bet' + D7 + scorecard M4/M5 establish rationale as the UNPROVEN load-bearing field for cross-tool resume. Architecture Open-Q#5 'compaction-proofing' (record re-injectable in full; keep top summary-dense, push detail into per-session files) already sanctions progressive disclosure, so a summary-dense default + view:full is the product's own principle, not an improvisation. Optimizing M3/M6 while holding M1/M4/M5 makes this a core scorecard axis, and a leaner record serves the prime directive 'integration cost must approach zero' (the moat). User approved step-by-step and delegated testing.
- 2026-07-08T13:21:57.981Z — chose **Keep digest-by-default BUT add a compact rejected-approaches ledger (each decision's `over` + brief `because`) to the digest — validated as the fix. Ablation on harness-build: 3 trap proposals each explicitly rejected in the record; digest arm (rejections absent) walked into the counterintuitive P3 (BD22 'hooks best-effort, never fail loudly') 2/2 AND invented a justification contradicting BD22, while full-JSON and readable arms (rejections present) declined all three 4/4, each citing the exact decision. Digest avoided P1/P2 only because general engineering knowledge covered them.** over Shipping the digest default as-is (unbounded M4 risk on exactly the class D7 bets on), reverting to full-by-default (loses the 93% token win), or padding the digest with the whole decision history (re-bloats it). because This empirically confirms D7 / scorecard M4-M5: rationale is load-bearing precisely where the model's prior is WRONG, and the digest's omission there produced a confidently-wrong re-proposal (the 'confidently wrong 72%' failure the spine names). The readable arm (rejections present) restored M4 to 4/4, and full-JSON performed identically to readable, so view:full is a sound escape hatch — omission, not format, is the issue. A rejected-approaches ledger is far cheaper than the full state (est ~0.5-1.5k tok vs ~15k) and protects the bet while keeping most of the token win.
- 2026-07-10T05:04:05.334Z — chose **Rejected-approaches ledger design: add a 'Rejected approaches' section to renderStatus (shared by the SessionStart injection AND the get_state digest) listing the `over` clause of every decision that has a real alternative — one terse clipped line each, over-only (no because) to start — budgeted, with the existing 10k hard cap as backstop. Build over-only first, then re-run the ablation P3 trap to confirm M4 is restored; escalate to include a short because only if over-only underperforms.** over Full chose/over/because for all decisions (re-bloats toward the 15k full state), only recent-N rejected approaches (the failing trap BD22 was OLD, so recency-capping re-breaks M4), or relying on section-fetch (Phase-3 showed agents don't proactively fetch). because Phase-3 ablation: the digest fails M4 only when a rejected approach is ABSENT; the readable arm (all rejected approaches present) restored M4 to 4/4. `over` clauses are short (~50 chars), so listing all is compact (~2.3k chars/~575 tok for 59 decisions) — breadth over depth. Placing it in renderStatus also protects the auto-injected SessionStart resume path, which faces the same trap.
- 2026-07-10T12:58:19.575Z — chose **Lean tool-definition pass = conservative description trims only: dedupe redundancy between tool and property descriptions (dropped log_decision prop descs, get_state view detail, planSchema desc), shorten the 6×-repeated initiative prop, tighten wording — while KEEPING the load-bearing semantics verbatim in spirit (get_state 'call this first', session_id adopt-by-id line, update_plan 'full replace, not a merge'). 4,615→3,992 chars, ~1,154→~998 est tok (−13.5%).** over Aggressive minification (dropping minLength/enum hints, one-line descriptions everywhere) for a bigger cut, or JSON-Schema $defs to dedupe shared shapes, or leaving definitions untouched because 1.15k tok is already small. because Tool definitions are injected into EVERY session that connects the server, so chars here are the highest-frequency read surface — but they are also the agent's usage contract: Phase-3 ablation proved omission (not format) is what breaks behavior, and the adopt-by-id + full-replace warnings each prevent a known field failure (orphaned hook sessions; silent plan merges). $defs would ADD bytes (each nested shape is used once) and some clients render them poorly. Enums were already enforced everywhere applicable (status, view), so the win had to come from description dedupe alone.
- 2026-07-10T13:03:42.665Z — chose **Phase-6 compact serialization lands as: (a) keep the digest's one-line list format — measured on this repo's live record, markdown tables LOSE (phases 482ch table vs 419ch list, +15%; decisions 1,448ch vs 1,414ch — header/separator/pipe overhead exceeds label savings); (b) collapse done phases into one trailing summary line (phases section 419→245ch, −42%, compounds with age, frees cap slots for open phases); (c) keep repo.md INLINE in the SessionStart block.** over Rendering tasks/decisions as terse markdown tables (the 6.1 premise as written), or replacing the inline repo.md section with a lazy "read .sofar/repo.md" pointer (the second half of 6.2). because Tables were measured, not assumed — the current list serialization is already the terse form, so 6.1 closes as evaluated-and-declined with numbers. The lazy repo.md pointer fails on Phase-3's central finding: agents do NOT proactively fetch, so a pointer would effectively delete repo conventions (test commands, push policy, boundaries) from orientation — an M5-consistency regression to save ~380 tok, exactly the omission-class failure the ablation flagged. Collapsing done phases is the one trim that is pure win: detail survives in plan.md, and the digest's job is orienting on what is NEXT, not history.
- 2026-07-10T14:20:41.880Z — chose **Descope 2.3 (on-demand section fetch, paginated) and close Phase 2 — user ratified. The digest + view:"full" + on-disk projections ARE the progressive-disclosure surface.** over Building a section/page parameter on sofar_get_state (the original 2.3), or keeping 2.3 parked in the plan indefinitely. because Phase-3 ablation showed agents don't proactively fetch detail, so the surface would go unused exactly when it matters; both deep-dive paths are already served (view:"full" for the complete state, generated projections readable from disk with zero tool calls); and a section/page param would re-grow the get_state schema the Phase-5 lean pass just trimmed — every session pays for a parameter's definition whether or not it is called.
- 2026-07-10T14:57:16.161Z — chose **Phase-0 M4 scoring convention (pre-registered before the SaaS arms, user-confirmed): a dead-end recurrence counts only if the session proposes/attempts a recorded-rejected approach WITHOUT acknowledging the recorded decision; informed re-tests that cite the decision and serve a new open question do not count. Applied symmetrically to all arms including B/gstack. R01 C-model accordingly rules M4=Pass (BD51 re-test cited the decision, predicted the E403, resolved D13); M6 = ~2min, staleness=Y, friction=1.** over The strict-letter reading of the frozen rubric ("propose or attempt it again" = fail regardless of awareness), which would have scored R01 M4=Fail(1) because M4's construct is record-transmission failure (D7): the failure mode is ignorant re-walking, which R02-iso reproduced (C-abl justified the BD16-rejected approach unaware). R01's re-test was the opposite — the record demonstrably transmitted (BD51 cited, failure predicted) and the attempt executed the spine's open D13 directive, producing new information that closed it. Pre-registering the refinement BEFORE the gate-deciding A/B runs, and recording both readings for R01, keeps the frozen-rubric discipline intact while scoring the construct rather than the letter.
- 2026-07-11T09:26:15.964Z — chose **Founder spot-check (mitigation b) CLEARED — all five convention calls ratified against raw evidence: R01 BD51 informed-re-test M4 ruling, R03 P5.1 non-recurrence ruling, the M2 exclusion split (human actions/preferences never count; re-asking a settled decision always counts), the GLM record-hole reading of M2 (handoff-bench anchors ratified as-is), and the M3 boundary (targeted spot-verification=1 vs broad re-verification=2 — the gate's M3 win stands). All three judge-provenance mitigations now discharged.** over Leaving mitigation (b) as an open footnote on the validation story, or amending the shipped M2 anchors toward the Fable reading (any-question-counts) because The one participant who isn't a language model reviewed the calls the machines converged on; M2's purpose is measuring record holes, not general human-dependence, so the GLM reading matches the metric's intent. Every check in the validation story is now cleared by both machine cross-examination and human review.
- 2026-07-11T10:02:46.158Z — chose **Add `sofar upgrade [version]` (flags --check/--dry-run/--force): derive the real npm prefix from the running binary path (fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) -> …/lib/node_modules/@alignlabs/sofar -> prefix), then npm install -g --prefix <prefix> @alignlabs/sofar@<target>. Non-global/npx/source layouts print manual guidance and never spawn npm. Pure core (planUpgrade/resolveUpgrade) unit-tested; spawn+registry behind injected deps.** over Telling users to run npm by hand, or a self-updater that trusts `npm config get prefix` because npm config prefix (/usr/local) disagrees with where sofar actually lives (~/.local), so a naive npm i -g installs to the wrong root and leaves the on-PATH copy stale. The running file path is ground truth for the prefix. 19 new tests, full suite 372 green, build clean, verified on the real bundle via a simulated global layout.
